commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=22b85dd29713febd2eb60ae19b539150db105afb)
commands.review_summary:
The branch migrates bundled Discord, Telegram, and related channel/session transcript callers to scoped accessor APIs while adding a public SDK transcript runtime subpath, docs/export metadata, and focused tests.

PR surface: Source +1251, Tests +874, Docs +3, Config +4, Generated 0, Other +4. Total +2136 across 57 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection of PR head shows scoped identity is passed outside the update payload while current Gateway live-message routing depends on sessionKey and agentId in the emitted transcript update.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Manual maintainer review is needed because the protected draft changes public SDK/session-state boundaries, has a concrete message-delivery finding, overlaps active contract PRs, and lacks contributor real behavior proof.

target.title: refactor: migrate bundled transcript target lookups
target.body:
## What

Refreshes the remaining Path 3 3.1b bundled transcript target lookup slice against current `main`. The PR migrates the remaining bundled Discord and Telegram transcript target lookups to scoped transcript runtime APIs while preserving current file-backed behavior where it is still a contract.

## Why

The audit found this slice is still independently needed after #95030, #95087, #89912, #89518, and #90439. Those merged PRs added identity/update/mirror seams, but current `main` still had bundled Discord and Telegram call sites resolving transcript targets through direct session-store/file helpers.

Refs #88838.

## Changes

- Add scoped SDK helpers
- Migrate Discord final lookup
- Migrate Telegram mirror/final lookup
- Stabilize Telegram mirror idempotency
- Preserve command file context
- Refresh API baseline
- Update focused tests

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `src/plugin-sdk/session-transcript-runtime.ts` | Adds scoped latest, guarded mirror, and legacy file-target helpers |
| `extensions/discord/src/monitor/message-handler.process.ts` | Reads transcript-backed finals through scoped reverse lookup |
| `extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.ts` | Uses guarded scoped mirror appends, per-message idempotency, and scoped final lookup |
| `extensions/telegram/src/bot-native-commands.ts` | Preserves legacy plugin-command `sessionFile` via explicit scoped helper |
| `docs/.generated/plugin-sdk-api-baseline.sha256` | Refreshes public SDK API baseline |
| `*.test.ts` | Covers mirror guards, repeats, injected session deps, and legacy command file context |

## Testing

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/discord/src/monitor/message-handler.process.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-native-commands.session-meta.test.ts src/plugin-sdk/session-transcript-runtime.test.ts src/plugin-sdk/session-transcript-hit.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts --reporter=verbose`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.extensions.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions.tsbuildinfo`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.extensions.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions-test.tsbuildinfo`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core.tsbuildinfo`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo`
- `pnpm plugin-sdk:api:check`
- `node scripts/plugin-sdk-surface-report.mjs --check`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.tooling.config.ts --reporter=verbose test/scripts/plugin-sdk-surface-report.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.tooling.config.ts --reporter=verbose` (278 files, 4386 tests)
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check ...`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs ...`
- `git diff --check`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base upstream/main`

Autoreview is clean after accepted findings were fixed.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: PR #89911 is refreshed at `3f74951260a23c759e4d1b2525f9826ddd6344e3` on current `main` (`fcedd37067ec7e5f6b7a1d35d2f68481622fa4b6`) and keeps the Path 3 3.1b bundled Discord/Telegram transcript target lookup migration scoped to identity-based transcript runtime helpers. This final head also resolves the latest `main` API-surface budget conflict and fixes the Telegram transcript mirror idempotency collision found by autoreview.

Real environment tested: Local macOS OpenClaw LaunchAgent from `/Users/phaedrus/Projects/clawdbot`, checkout detached at `3f74951260a`, Gateway `OpenClaw 2026.6.9 (3f74951)`, loopback port `18789`, configured `main` agent, real `openai/gpt-5.5` agent turn. The live checkout is intentionally left detached at the PR head so the running gateway stays loaded with this patch.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
- `git fetch upstream clawdbot-d02.1.9.1.18.1/31b-bundled-transcript-target-lookup && git switch --detach 3f74951260a23c759e4d1b2525f9826ddd6344e3`
- `pnpm build`
- `pnpm openclaw gateway restart`
- `pnpm openclaw gateway health --json`
- `pnpm openclaw gateway call health --json`
- `pnpm openclaw gateway status --json`
- `curl -fsS http://127.0.0.1:18789/healthz`
- `curl -fsS http://127.0.0.1:18789/readyz`
- `pnpm openclaw --version`
- `pnpm openclaw agent --agent main --session-key agent:main:path3-proof-89911-3f74951 --message "Path 3 PR 89911 conflict-resolution local gateway proof. Reply with exactly: PATH3_89911_3F74951_GATEWAY_OK" --json --timeout 180`
- `pnpm openclaw gateway call sessions.describe --params '{"key":"agent:main:path3-proof-89911-3f74951"}' --json`
- `pnpm openclaw gateway call sessions.preview --params '{"keys":["agent:main:path3-proof-89911-3f74951"],"limit":4,"maxChars":2000}' --json`
- `tail -n 12 /Users/phaedrus/.openclaw/agents/main/sessions/89ec9598-0ad1-4ebd-8057-f1901eaa7674.jsonl`

Evidence after fix:
- `pnpm build` passed on the final PR head.
- `dist/build-info.json` recorded commit `3f74951260a23c759e4d1b2525f9826ddd6344e3`.
- `pnpm openclaw --version` reported `OpenClaw 2026.6.9 (3f74951)`.
- `/healthz` returned `{"ok":true,"status":"live"}`.
- `/readyz` returned `{"ready":true,"failing":[]...}`.
- `gateway status --json` returned `rpc.ok: true`, server version `2026.6.9`, and no plugin version drift.
- The live agent run returned `status: "ok"`, run id `e71489b4-ff89-483f-9b6d-26c46b8639d6`, session id `89ec9598-0ad1-4ebd-8057-f1901eaa7674`, provider `openai`, model `gpt-5.5`, and final visible text `PATH3_89911_3F74951_GATEWAY_OK`.
- `sessions.describe` returned key `agent:main:path3-proof-89911-3f74951`, session id `89ec9598-0ad1-4ebd-8057-f1901eaa7674`, status `done`, model `gpt-5.5`, runtime `openclaw`.
- `sessions.preview` returned the expected user prompt and assistant text `PATH3_89911_3F74951_GATEWAY_OK` for the same key.
- The backing transcript JSONL contains the same user row and final assistant row for `PATH3_89911_3F74951_GATEWAY_OK`.

Observed result after fix: The refreshed PR branch builds, starts the local Gateway, resolves the dedicated session by scoped key through Gateway APIs, and reads back the persisted transcript content produced by the live agent turn.

What was not tested: I did not send an external Telegram or Discord message from the live gateway. The channel-specific Discord/Telegram caller behavior is covered by focused tests in this PR; the live proof avoids mutating external chat channels while still loading the patched Gateway and proving scoped session/transcript readback in the real runtime.
